expect: >=1 cross-domain principle from structural enforcement convergence across 3+ domains. F-NK6+F-SWARMER1 share L-601 dependency.
actual: L-1165 written: scale-dependent intervention matching. Two intervention classes (additive vs multiplicative) with opposite efficacy at large N. Additive fails at N>500 (marginal weight <0.2%); multiplicative succeeds immediately by changing selection pressure. Convergent across 3 domains (NK, expert-swarm, meta). Additional: 33% false-instrument rate across 9 experiments.
diff: Expected >=1 principle: CONFIRMED. Found 2 independent patterns: (1) additive-vs-multiplicative intervention matching (novel), (2) 33% false-instrument rate (extends L-1091/L-1145). Both are L3 (architectural). Unexpected: the scale-dependent crossover aligns with P-287 integration-bound waypoint (N~550).

cross_domain_patterns.structural_enforcement_universality: L-601 appears in all 9 experiments but manifestation is domain-specific: NK=creation-time, EXPSW=proportional scoring, CAT=advisory-only, RAND=criterion revision
cross_domain_patterns.measurement_false_instrument_rate: 3/9 (33%) found measurement tool/criterion wrong: SWARMER1 calibration, KNOW1 score ranking, RAND1 Gini criterion
cross_domain_patterns.resolution_mode_produces_L3: F-NK6, F-KNOW1, F-ISO2 all in resolution mode produced L3 insights
